meals.name: Savory Teff Cakes with Wilted Chard
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups teff ( whole grain, not flour)
1 teaspoon Herbs de Provence or Italian seasoning
1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved or sliced
1/4 cup Italian parsley ( or cilantro or basil) chopped
meals.recipe:
Cook the teff. In a medium pot, heat oil over medium heat. Add shallot and saute until fragrant and deeply golden. Add water, teff, salt, spices and whisk until smooth. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover and cook 15 minutes, or until teff is tender (yet still intact) and when stirred has a consistency of a thick porridge.
Grease an 8×8 inch baking dish with olive oil. Give teff a good stir and pour into the baking dish. Using an oiled spatula, smooth the top. Let this cool and place into the fridge until it sets up and is firm, about 4 hours (or overnight). If making ahead see notes.
Once teff is cooled and firm, cut into four squares. Remove cakes, then cut each into two triangles. ( See notes, for appetizer version.)
Pan fry the teff cakes in a skillet, with a little oil, over medium heat, until crispy and heated through, about 5-6 minutes on each side. Place in a warm oven if you like. (Alternately, you can bake these on a greased, parchment-lined sheet pan- 20 mins @400F . Brush the tops with olive oil. )
Wipe out the skillet, heat 1 tablespoon oil over medium heat and add garlic, stirring until golden, about 2-3 mintues. Add Kale and gently wilt. Add a splash of white wine and season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
Place tomatoes, parsley, olive oil, and balsamic in a small bowl and mix. Season with salt and pepper.
Place the warm, crispy teff cakes ( 2 triangles per person) on a plate, layering with the chard. Divide the tomato relish over the top, spooning any remaining dressing on or around the cakes. Serve immediately!

meals.name: Homemade Vegetarian Chili Recipe
ingredients: 1/2 cup roasted red pepper, drained from jar
1 can kidney beans, drained and rinsed
4 tablespoons orange juice or apple cider vinegar
meals.recipe:
In a saucepot on medium-high heat, add avocado oil or olive oil, minced garlic, diced onion, celery, and carrot. Stirring occasionally cook for 3 to 5 minutes or until the onions begin to brown.
Once the meat and vegetables have browned, add in the spices and roasted red peppers and cook for 1 minute until aromatic and stir to combine. Stir in the crumbled Trifecta meat patties and cook for 3 minutes or add 16 ounces of beyond meat and cook for 5 minutes.
Continue to cook on medium-high heat and add vegetable stock to release any of the brown bits stuck to the pan, then stir in the diced tomatoes and beans; bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer cook for 10 minutes. Season with orange juice or apple cider vinegar, a pinch of salt or pepper is desired then dig in!

meals.name: Tempeh Garlic Ginger Soy Bowl Recipe
ingredients: 16 ounces Tempeh, diced into bite sizes
1 cup Trifecta mixed veg or broccoli
1 avocado, sliced lengthwise, divided into quarters
meals.recipe:
For sauce: In a small saucepan, heat 2 teaspoons of avocado oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and ginger, and stir often for about 1 minute or until a dark golden color begins to form. Add red pepper flakes, Chinese five-spice, and stir for another 30 seconds, until spices are bright. Immediately add the tamari, stir and bring to an aggressive simmer for 3-5 minutes. As the sauce simmers, mix cornstarch and water in a small bowl to make a slurry. Add slurry into the sauce and simmer...
Heat remaining 1 teaspoon of avocado oil in a large non-stick saute pan over medium-high heat. Add 1 teaspoon of garlic, stir to avoid garlic from burning, and immediately add diced seitan. Mix often. The goal with this step is to sear and develop a golden brown color for the seitan. Once the seitan has caramelized, add the sauce to the pan and stir. Add the Trifecta bell peppers. Coat seitan and veggies evenly and cook for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and keep warm until needed.
Warm up the rice in the microwave for about 2-2:30 minutes, along with the Trifecta broccoli.
Portioning in 4 bowls, plate in each ½ cup of Trifecta rice, ¼ cup of broccoli, and 4.5 ounces of tempeh mix. Garnish with ¼ avocado per bowl, sesame seeds and fresh cilantro.

meals.name: Ethiopian Wild Rice Pilaf
ingredients: 2 medium leeks (white and light green parts), diced and rinsed
¾ teaspoon berbere spice Blend, or to taste
2 cups cooked adzuki beans, or one 15-ounce can, drained and rinsed
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
4 green onions (white and green parts), thinly sliced
meals.recipe:
Place the leeks in a large saucepan, and sauté over medium heat for 10 minutes. Add water 1 to 2 tablespoons at a time to keep the leeks from sticking to the pan.
Add the garlic and cook for 2 minutes. Add the berbere spice and cook for 30 seconds. Stir in the wild rice, beans, and orange zest, and season with salt and pepper. Cook until the mixture is heated through.
Garnish with the green onions.

meals.name: Ethiopian Spris
meals.recipe:
Cut the papaya in half and then into 1/4. Chop the 1/4 section and set it aside.
Cut and chop the mangoes and avocadoes and set them aside.
Place each of the fruits into three large different cups to blend with an immersion blender.
Add 1/3 cup of sugar to each cup, 1/3 cup of cold water, 1/3 cup of ice, and 1 tablespoon of lime juice. Blend it until smooth. All three juices should be smooth and creamy.
To layer, start by spooning the papaya mixture into a glass. Next, spoon the avocado mixture, and finally, spoon the mango mixture on top. You do not want to simply pour the mixtures on top on top of each other as they can sometimes sink into each other.
Garnish with lime zest and mint if desired. Enjoy!

meals.name: Vegan Savory Grilled Tofu, Broccoli, and Rice Bowl
ingredients: 1 can black beans, drained and rinsed
meals.recipe:
Evenly slice your tofu into strips (about 2 ounces each), pat dry and season with half of the nutritional yeast and pepper.
Add cooking spray on a non-stick grill pan and warm to medium-high heat. Once ready, add your tofu and grill for 3 minutes on each side until it starts to crisp up and leave grill marks.
Add your rice, drained beans, and broccoli to a large mixing bowl. Season with oil, remaining nutritional yeast, black pepper, and salt.
Portion out your rice mixture onto four plates or meal prep containers and top each with 3 pieces of grilled tofu.
Garnish with herbs and a sprinkle of salt.
Enjoy!

meals.name: Nichole Ohrablo's Super Shake Recipe
meals.recipe:
Add all ingredients to blender
Blend until smooth
Serve and enjoy

meals.name: Easy Beefy Flatbread Recipe
ingredients: ½ cup store-bought slaw mix or thinly sliced cabbage (use a mandolin)
meals.recipe:
Preheat toaster oven or larger oven to 350F. Slightly toast the flatbreads to help them keep their bodies and avoid becoming soggy while constructing them. Spray a sheet tray with oil and spread all four flatbreads on the tray, making sure they are not overlapping each other. Toast for 3 minutes, keeping an eye on the flatbreads. Remove from the oven carefully. Keep oven on.
Microwave the Trifecta beef patties covered completely by a damp paper cloth towel, for a minute and a half. Remove from microwave and after the patties are slightly cooled down, crumble the patties to its original form - ground beef - with your hands into a separate bowl. Keep warm.
Spread 1 tablespoon of pesto on top of each flatbread.
Divide ¼ cup of arugula and ¼ cup of Trifecta mushrooms on top of each flatbread, sprinkling around.
Similarly, sprinkle 4 ounces of the crumbled beef on each flatbread. Finish by dividing the 1 ounce of crumbled gorgonzola cheese evenly into all 4 flatbreads (¼ ounce on each).
Return built flatbreads to the sheet tray used to toast the flatbreads, and bake in the oven for 3-5 minutes, or until cheese is melted. Cut into quarters and enjoy!

meals.name: Vegan Low-Carb Breakfast Taco Recipe
ingredients: pinch black pepper and sea salt
meals.recipe:
Crumble tofu into a large bowl and add spices.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat for 1 minute then add oil.
Add tofu to the hot skillet and cook for 5-8 minutes until golden and firm. Finish with Lime juice.
To Build Taco: Gently place the tofu filling into your jicama tortilla and top with 1-2 Tablespoons of pico or salsa along with fresh cilantro.

meals.name: Ethiopian Cabbage
ingredients: ½ cup (120ml) olive oil (or canola oil)
½ teaspoon (1g) cayenne pepper ((more or less to taste))
8 cups (600g) chopped cabbage ((about ½ cabbage head))
meals.recipe:
Heat oil over medium heat in a large saucepan or Dutch oven.
Add the onions and let them saute for a minute or two, then add the carrots, potatoes, and tomatoes.
Stir in the spices: garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, curry, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Simmer for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
Pour about 1 cup of water into the saucepan (add more as needed). Simmer for 10-15 minutes until the potatoes are almost tender.
Finally, add cabbage and green pepper, stir, and continue cooking for about 5 minutes.
Adjust for seasonings. Serve hot.

meals.name: Vegan BBQ Tofu Stir Fry Recipe
ingredients: 1 tablespoon Tamari (gluten-free soy sauce), low sodium preferred
3 each Garlic, fresh, grated or 2 teaspoon garlic powder
1 teaspoon Ginger, fresh, grated, or 3 teaspoons ginger powder
meals.recipe:
Prepare the BBQ sauce by combining all ingredients in a bowl. Whisk to incorporate. Keep on the side.
Drain tofu and press by placing paper towels in the top and bottom of the tofu and placing a hefty book carefully on top of the tofu. Use a small sheet tray if needed to capture the water drainage. Press for at least 5 minutes, discard paper towels, and then dice block evenly. Transfer to a bowl. Season tofu with cornstarch, 2 tablespoons of the BBQ sauce, and a pinch of salt. Mix with hands to coat tofu evenly. Set aside.
Heat up avocado oil on a non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Once the oil is slightly smoking (about 30-45 seconds of constant heat), add the tofu. You want to hear a sear go on as we are looking at caramelizing the tofu and browning it on most sides. Do this for about 2-3 minutes, stirring as necessary. Add the vegetables, along with the quinoa and brown rice. Saute for another 2-3 minutes until heated through. Lastly, add the sauce into the pan and mix to coat all ingredients evenly. The s...
Conversely, if you are tracking your macros closely, heat up the quinoa separately in a bowl in the microwave, covered with a damp paper towel for 2 minutes. Do the same with the Trifecta vegetables. Still reheat the tofu in the pan by searing it over medium-high heat with avocado oil and the sauce.
Portion ⅔ cup of quinoa, ½ cup of Trifecta veggies, and 4 ounce of the saucy tofu on a bowl. Garnish with fresh herbs like cilantro, and some sesame seeds.
Enjoy!

meals.name: Injera
ingredients: 1 cup (120g) corn flour (or a fine cornmeal (not cornstarch))
½ cup (70g) sorghum (or whole wheat flour)
meals.recipe:
Easy Yeast Method
Quick Method
Cooking

meals.name: Roasted Paleo Cauliflower with Agrodolce Sauce Recipe
meals.recipe:
Preheat oven to 425 F. Cut the cauliflower into florets and transfer it to a foil-lined baking sheet pan. Season with kosher salt and 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Roast for 15 minutes. Set a timer.
Meanwhile, combine sliced rounds of Fresno chiles, red wine vinegar, olive oil, honey, red pepper flakes, and raisins in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil and then reduce heat to a slow simmer to allow the sauce to thicken up for 20 minutes. Remove from heat once thickened.
Remove cauliflower from the oven once done roasting and transfer into a bowl. Pour agrodolce sauce over the cauliflower and toss to coat. Serve immediately or cool down completely if using for meal prep.

meals.name: Vegan Balsamic Mushroom Avocado Toast Recipe
ingredients: 2 slices millet-chia GF bread (or 1 slice sprouted wheat bread)
½ cup sliced shiitake mushrooms or 1/2 cup
meals.recipe:
Preheat an oven to 400 degrees and toast bread for 3-5 minutes depending on personal preference. Or toast in a toaster oven.
In a small bowl, mash the avocado and combine with the lime juice and sea salt, then set aside.
Preheat a saute pan over medium-high heat for 1 minute. Add chopped mushrooms and tempeh, saute for 3 minutes stirring frequently. After 3 minutes add half of the water to "steam" and continue to saute.
Saute for 3 more minutes, then add the balsamic, coconut aminos, and lemon juice. Cook for 2 more minutes or until the liquids have formed a glaze over the mushrooms and tempeh. Remove from heat.
To build your Toast: spread the mashed avocado onto the toasted bread and top with balsamic glazed mushroom and tempeh. Add arugula dressed lightly in lemon juice or kale for extra crunch and flavor.

meals.name: Simple Quinoa Bowl Recipe
meals.recipe:
Warm quinoa in a microwave for about 1 minute, until lightly warmed (close to room temperature). Add to a large mixing bowl, stirring to break it up.
Drain broccoli and add to the bowl. Chop any large pieces into smaller, bite-sized pieces.
Coarsely chop almonds and add to the bowl.
Chop scallions and parsley and add to quinoa.
Chop figs into small pieces and add to the mixture.
Season with olive oil and lemon juice.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Finish with nutritional yeast and enjoy!

meals.name: No-Bake Gluten-Free High Protein Energy Bites Recipe
ingredients: 2 Tbsp Maple Syrup or Raw Honey
meals.recipe:
In large bowl, combine oats, ground chia, hemp seeds, cacao powder, protein powder, cinnamon and sea salt
Add in almond butter, maple syrup, vanilla and optional mix-ins, mix until just combined
Scoop 1 tbsp of mixture and roll between hands into ball
Place on cookie sheet and repeat until all mixture is used (makes about 20-28)
Place cookie sheet in refrigerator for about 30 minutes to set
Store in airtight container in refrigerator for up to 1 week

meals.name: Paleo Spiced Roasted Butternut Squash Recipe
ingredients: 1 whole butternut squash, stems removed, peeled, and seeded
meals.recipe:
Preheat oven to 450F. Cut both ends, peel the butternut squash, and seed it. Discard skin and seeds. Cut the squash into small evenly-sized rectangles and transfer to a sheet tray lined with foil.
Mix the spice mix, including the salt, in a small bowl. Season the butternut squash liberally with the spice mix and add the oil. Mix with hands to fully incorporate.
Roast in the preheated oven for 35 minutes. Pierce with a fork to test doneness. There should be no resistance. If there is, add 10 minutes and continue roasting.
Remove from oven, cool down and store in an airtight container, or serve 3 oz per dish for exact macros. Conversely, if you are serving this for dinner, serve immediately with your preferred protein and paleo vegetables.

meals.name: Teff Porridge
ingredients: generous 3-finger pinch of and ground warming spice- like ground clove, cardamom, nutmeg, or cinnamon, allspice or even 5 spice.
generous 3 finger pinch of kosher salt
fresh fruit- sliced figs or berries or pears
2 tablespoons crushed, toasted walnuts- or any other nut.
meals.recipe:
Over medium-high heat, In a small pot, toast teff until it begins to pop, and smell nutty- stirring often, about 3 minutes.
Once you hear its adorable popping, add water and spices and salt.
Bring to a boil, cover and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes. Give a quick stir and at this point,
If it seems too thick, add a bit more water, to get it to your desired consistency. Cook a couple more minutes.
Divide among 2 bowls and top with sliced figs, walnuts and a generous drizzle of honey.

meals.name: High Protein Pesto Pasta Recipe
ingredients: High Protein pasta made from chickpea, peas, or lentils
½ Cup Sun-dried tomatoes, packed in oil, drained
For the Pesto: (makes about 1 ½ cups)
1 Cup Packed parsley leaves, stems on!
⅓ Cup Olive oil or avocado oil (less bitter)
meals.recipe:
Prepare Pasta According to Packaged Instructions, remember to reserve about ¼ cup of the pasta water. Return the empty pot to the stove and set cooked pasta aside.
For the Pesto: In the base of a food processor add all of the ingredients. Pulse until blended thoroughly, add additional lemon juice or apple cider vinegar if needed for consistency.
Preheat the empty pasta pot over medium heat. Add the artichokes and Sundried tomatoes. Cook for 2 minutes. Add the Spinach and cook slightly, about 1-2 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the cooked pasta, pesto, and pasta water, stir, and plate.
Garnish with vegan parmesan or toasted sunflower seeds if desired.

meals.name: Vegan Meatballs & Spaghetti Squash Pasta Recipe
ingredients: 1 (25 oz) Jar Tomato Basil Pasta Sauce, store-bought or homemade
Pinch of Black Pepper and Sea Salt
meals.recipe:
Preheat an oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit. Place spaghetti squash cut-side down on a foil-lined sheet tray and roast for 25 minutes.
Remove from the oven and let sit on the baking tray for 5 minutes. Using a fork, scrape out the inside spaghetti and set aside.
Place the beyond beef into a medium bowl, then combine all other meatball ingredients into a food processor, pulse till coarsely chopped. Mix with the beyond beef and form 1-inch meatballs.
Pre-heat a medium-sized non-stick skillet over medium heat, add oil.
Sear meatballs in the pan for 5 minutes, turning occasionally. Add Pasta Sauce and simmer for 20 minutes.
To plate: combine with spaghetti squash and top with vegan parmesan, red pepper flakes, and fresh herbs.

meals.name: Low-Carb Portobello Mushroom Bruschetta
meals.recipe:
First, heat up a skillet or a grill. Then spray the Mushrooms with non stick spray. Grill for 2-3 minutes on each side. Remove from heat but keep the stove/ grill on.
In a bowl, mix the tomatoes, garlic, balsamic vinegar, and basil together. Add the mix to the inside of the mushrooms.
Top the mushrooms with cheese and place back on the skillet/grill.
Cover and heat for 2-3 minutes or until the cheese has melted. Enjoy!

meals.name: Chocolate Banana Berry Protein Shake Recipe
meals.recipe:
Place all ingredients into a blender
Blend until smooth
Serve immediately

meals.name: Low Carb Berry Cobbler Crumble Recipe
meals.recipe:
Preheat oven to 375 degrees F
Toss berries with xanthum gum, lemon zest and any sweetener, if using
Put berries in a cast iron skillet or oven safe dish
Bake for 15 minutes
While berries are baking, mix together almond flour, protein powder and 1 tbsp melted coconut oil until crumbly
After 15 minutes, remove berries from oven and top with crumble mixture
Bake 3-5 minutes or until golden
Serve topped with whipped cream or greek yogurt

meals.name: Ethiopian Injera Bread
meals.recipe:
In a large bowl, mix both flours, salt, and baking soda together. Whisk in the club soda until smooth. Then add the vinegar and whisk.
Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Pour oil on a paper towel and wipe the skillet with the oiled paper towel. This creates a thin fatty layer to make it easier to flip the crepes.
Using a scoop, pour batter into the skillet creating a 6-inch circle. Quickly and carefully swirl the pan around to thin out the batter until it measures 8- to 9-inches across.
Cook for 1 minute, then using a large spatula, flip the Injera over and cook another minute. Remove from the skillet and stack on a plate. Repeat with remaining batter. The Injera will seem slightly crisp in the pan, but will soften immediately when placed on the plate. The stacking also helps steam and soften the Injera sourdough crepes.
Once finished cooking the Injera. Cut the circles in half with a pizza cutter, roll into tubes and stack. Keep warm until ready to serve. Serve the Injera with Doro Wat or Mesir Wat, tearing pieces of Injera and using it to pick up the Doro Wat.

meals.name: Azefa - Ethiopian Lentil Salad
meals.recipe:
Bring lentils and 4 cups of water to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer and cook for 30 minutes, until lentils are just tender.
Drain immediately and run with cold water.
Using a coffee/spice grinder, grind mustard seed, black pepper and cayenne pepper to a fine powder.
Transfer the spice powder to a bowl and add boiling water. Stir to combine.
Add lemon juice and slowly drizzle in olive oil, while whisking constantly.
In a large bowl, combine lentils, bell pepper, cilantro and salt.
Add dressing and stir to combine.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.

meals.name: Vegan Tofu Tabbouleh Bowl Recipe
ingredients: 3-4 small beets, roasted at home, or store-bought sliced
or 1 package of firm tofu cut into cubes
meals.recipe:
Prepare your fried tofu, tabbouleh, and quinoa according to recipe directions. Combine the quinoa and 2 cups tabbouleh together to marinade. You should have extra tabbouleh for finishing your bowl.
Wash your vegetables and greens. Remove the stems from the kale and finely chop. Using a vegetable peeler, peel the carrot into fine strips. Toss the kale, carrots, and chickpeas together and set aside.
Remove the peel from your orange and slice into slices. Do this over a bowl to save any orange juice and mix it into the tabbouleh.
To build your bowl: measure 1/3 cup tabbouleh and quinoa mixture, 4 ounces tofu, 1/2 cup roasted beets, 3-4 sliced orange, 1 cup kale, carrot, and chickpea salad. Top with extra tabbouleh if desired.

meals.name: Roasted Paleo Cauliflower with Agrodolce Sauce Recipe
meals.recipe:
Preheat oven to 425 F. Cut the cauliflower into florets and transfer it to a foil-lined baking sheet pan. Season with kosher salt and 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Roast for 15 minutes. Set a timer.
Meanwhile, combine sliced rounds of Fresno chiles, red wine vinegar, olive oil, honey, red pepper flakes, and raisins in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil and then reduce heat to a slow simmer to allow the sauce to thicken up for 20 minutes. Remove from heat once thickened.
Remove cauliflower from the oven once done roasting and transfer into a bowl. Pour agrodolce sauce over the cauliflower and toss to coat. Serve immediately or cool down completely if using for meal prep.

meals.name: Cheesy Vegan Tofu Scramble Recipe
ingredients: 14 ounces firm tofu, drained and pressed
½ cup Trifecta green bell pepper
½ cup Trifecta portobello mushrooms, diced
meals.recipe:
Crumble tofu into a large bowl.
Stir in spices and nutritional yeast, 1 to 2 Tbsp of olive oil, and let sit for five minutes.
Add crumbled tofu to the skillet and sauté for 5 minutes, until tofu is lightly golden and firm.
Add in veggies and stir for another 3 to 4 minutes, mixing flavors together until spinach is wilted.
Season with salt and pepper and serve immediately.

meals.name: Ethiopian Yataklete kilkil
ingredients: ¼ cup or more canola oil
½ teaspoon curry powder or turmeric spice
½ teaspoon white or black pepper
1 teaspoon salt, (plus more adjust to suit taste buds)
meals.recipe:
Add potatoes in a large saucepan, salt, and add water just enough to cover potatoes. Boil until tender - about 10 minutes or more. Remove and drain water.
In a large saucepan or Dutch oven, heat oil over medium heat. Add onions, give it a minute or two then stir in all the spices; garlic, Jalapenos peppers, ginger, coriander, smoked paprika, curry, white pepper, and chili pepper. Simmer for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burns. Then add tomatoes and bring to a boil and simmer for another 5 minutes.
Then add potatoes, green beans, and carrots. Season with salt.
Continue to simmer for about 10 minutes; stirring frequently to allow the flavors to blend. If needed, add water to prevent any burns.
Adjust for seasonings. Serve warm.

meals.name: Vegan Tofu Tikka Masala Recipe
ingredients: 16 ounces of store-bought baked tofu, or 1
Trifecta A la Carte white rice
meals.recipe:
Preheat the oven to 450 degrees fahrenheit. Trim the cauliflower into small florets and, in a bowl, mix together the cauliflower, coconut oil, nutritional yeast, garam masala, and lime juice. Spread out the cauliflower onto a baking tray lined with a reusable liner or parchment paper. Bake for 20 minutes, until slightly golden, then remove from the oven. Cut the baked tofu into medium-sized pieces or prepare one recipe fried tofu and set aside.
Prepare the tikka masala sauce
Add your roasted vegetables and tofu to the simmering tikka masala sauce and cook for 5 minutes, then remove from the heat.
Serve 1-1.5 cups of masala with 1/2 cup of rice and top with chopped cilantro.

meals.name: Curried Cauliflower Quinoa Recipe
meals.recipe:
Dice the scallion and bell pepper into small bite sized pieces. Then wash, de-stem and mince the parsley.
In a large mixing bowl, combine quinoa, cauliflower and chopped veggies. Add in raisins and parsley.
Stir in curry powder to evenly coat everything. Add some chili flakes to increase spiciness.
Add olive oil and lemon juice, and season with salt to taste.
Enjoy!

meals.name: Roasted Paleo Cauliflower with Agrodolce Sauce Recipe
meals.recipe:
Preheat oven to 425 F. Cut the cauliflower into florets and transfer it to a foil-lined baking sheet pan. Season with kosher salt and 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Roast for 15 minutes. Set a timer.
Meanwhile, combine sliced rounds of Fresno chiles, red wine vinegar, olive oil, honey, red pepper flakes, and raisins in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil and then reduce heat to a slow simmer to allow the sauce to thicken up for 20 minutes. Remove from heat once thickened.
Remove cauliflower from the oven once done roasting and transfer into a bowl. Pour agrodolce sauce over the cauliflower and toss to coat. Serve immediately or cool down completely if using for meal prep.

target_macros.rationale: Protein: 2.2 g/kg lean mass (bulk/recomp, BF% known). Fat: ~25% of calories (diet-mode aware; no somatotype tweak). Carbs: remainder of calorie budget unless constrained by diet mode (vegan).

water_guidance: Aim to be peeing clear by noon.
Have five clear urinations a day.
Don't be dehydrated at the time of your workouts.
Taper water toward the end of the day so you don't wake to pee.

protocol_notes: Calories are matched by portion scaling at the day level.
Protein is scored during meal selection, but add a lean protein side if a day falls short.
Diet/allergen filters are hard constraints; cuisine preference is a soft adherence preference.
Use weekly average adherence, weight, and measurements before changing calories.
Fruit/veg target: 3 cups fruit and 3 cups vegetables daily; fibre target ~29 g/day.

alternatives.name: Vegan Savory Grilled Tofu, Broccoli, and Rice Bowl
ingredients: 1 can black beans, drained and rinsed
alternatives.recipe:
Evenly slice your tofu into strips (about 2 ounces each), pat dry and season with half of the nutritional yeast and pepper.
Add cooking spray on a non-stick grill pan and warm to medium-high heat. Once ready, add your tofu and grill for 3 minutes on each side until it starts to crisp up and leave grill marks.
Add your rice, drained beans, and broccoli to a large mixing bowl. Season with oil, remaining nutritional yeast, black pepper, and salt.
Portion out your rice mixture onto four plates or meal prep containers and top each with 3 pieces of grilled tofu.
Garnish with herbs and a sprinkle of salt.
Enjoy!

Add olive oil and lemon juice, and season with salt to taste.
Enjoy!

alternatives.name: High Protein Pesto Pasta Recipe
ingredients: High Protein pasta made from chickpea, peas, or lentils
½ Cup Sun-dried tomatoes, packed in oil, drained
For the Pesto: (makes about 1 ½ cups)
1 Cup Packed parsley leaves, stems on!
⅓ Cup Olive oil or avocado oil (less bitter)
alternatives.recipe:
Prepare Pasta According to Packaged Instructions, remember to reserve about ¼ cup of the pasta water. Return the empty pot to the stove and set cooked pasta aside.
For the Pesto: In the base of a food processor add all of the ingredients. Pulse until blended thoroughly, add additional lemon juice or apple cider vinegar if needed for consistency.
Preheat the empty pasta pot over medium heat. Add the artichokes and Sundried tomatoes. Cook for 2 minutes. Add the Spinach and cook slightly, about 1-2 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the cooked pasta, pesto, and pasta water, stir, and plate.
Garnish with vegan parmesan or toasted sunflower seeds if desired.

alternatives.name: Vegan Balsamic Mushroom Avocado Toast Recipe
ingredients: 2 slices millet-chia GF bread (or 1 slice sprouted wheat bread)
½ cup sliced shiitake mushrooms or 1/2 cup
alternatives.recipe:
Preheat an oven to 400 degrees and toast bread for 3-5 minutes depending on personal preference. Or toast in a toaster oven.
In a small bowl, mash the avocado and combine with the lime juice and sea salt, then set aside.
Preheat a saute pan over medium-high heat for 1 minute. Add chopped mushrooms and tempeh, saute for 3 minutes stirring frequently. After 3 minutes add half of the water to "steam" and continue to saute.
Saute for 3 more minutes, then add the balsamic, coconut aminos, and lemon juice. Cook for 2 more minutes or until the liquids have formed a glaze over the mushrooms and tempeh. Remove from heat.
To build your Toast: spread the mashed avocado onto the toasted bread and top with balsamic glazed mushroom and tempeh. Add arugula dressed lightly in lemon juice or kale for extra crunch and flavor.

alternatives.name: Savory Teff Cakes with Wilted Chard
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups teff ( whole grain, not flour)
1 teaspoon Herbs de Provence or Italian seasoning
1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved or sliced
1/4 cup Italian parsley ( or cilantro or basil) chopped
alternatives.recipe:
Cook the teff. In a medium pot, heat oil over medium heat. Add shallot and saute until fragrant and deeply golden. Add water, teff, salt, spices and whisk until smooth. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover and cook 15 minutes, or until teff is tender (yet still intact) and when stirred has a consistency of a thick porridge.
Grease an 8×8 inch baking dish with olive oil. Give teff a good stir and pour into the baking dish. Using an oiled spatula, smooth the top. Let this cool and place into the fridge until it sets up and is firm, about 4 hours (or overnight). If making ahead see notes.
Once teff is cooled and firm, cut into four squares. Remove cakes, then cut each into two triangles. ( See notes, for appetizer version.)
Pan fry the teff cakes in a skillet, with a little oil, over medium heat, until crispy and heated through, about 5-6 minutes on each side. Place in a warm oven if you like. (Alternately, you can bake these on a greased, parchment-lined sheet pan- 20 mins @400F . Brush the tops with olive oil. )
Wipe out the skillet, heat 1 tablespoon oil over medium heat and add garlic, stirring until golden, about 2-3 mintues. Add Kale and gently wilt. Add a splash of white wine and season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
Place tomatoes, parsley, olive oil, and balsamic in a small bowl and mix. Season with salt and pepper.
Place the warm, crispy teff cakes ( 2 triangles per person) on a plate, layering with the chard. Divide the tomato relish over the top, spooning any remaining dressing on or around the cakes. Serve immediately!

alternatives.name: Teff Porridge
ingredients: generous 3-finger pinch of and ground warming spice- like ground clove, cardamom, nutmeg, or cinnamon, allspice or even 5 spice.
generous 3 finger pinch of kosher salt
fresh fruit- sliced figs or berries or pears
2 tablespoons crushed, toasted walnuts- or any other nut.
alternatives.recipe:
Over medium-high heat, In a small pot, toast teff until it begins to pop, and smell nutty- stirring often, about 3 minutes.
Once you hear its adorable popping, add water and spices and salt.
Bring to a boil, cover and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes. Give a quick stir and at this point,
If it seems too thick, add a bit more water, to get it to your desired consistency. Cook a couple more minutes.
Divide among 2 bowls and top with sliced figs, walnuts and a generous drizzle of honey.

alternatives.name: Ethiopian Wild Rice Pilaf
ingredients: 2 medium leeks (white and light green parts), diced and rinsed
¾ teaspoon berbere spice Blend, or to taste
2 cups cooked adzuki beans, or one 15-ounce can, drained and rinsed
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
4 green onions (white and green parts), thinly sliced
alternatives.recipe:
Place the leeks in a large saucepan, and sauté over medium heat for 10 minutes. Add water 1 to 2 tablespoons at a time to keep the leeks from sticking to the pan.
Add the garlic and cook for 2 minutes. Add the berbere spice and cook for 30 seconds. Stir in the wild rice, beans, and orange zest, and season with salt and pepper. Cook until the mixture is heated through.
Garnish with the green onions.

8 cups (600g) chopped cabbage ((about ½ cabbage head))

Pour about 1 cup of water into the saucepan (add more as needed). Simmer for 10-15 minutes until the potatoes are almost tender.

ingredients: 2 Tbsp Maple Syrup or Raw Honey
alternatives.recipe:
In large bowl, combine oats, ground chia, hemp seeds, cacao powder, protein powder, cinnamon and sea salt
Add in almond butter, maple syrup, vanilla and optional mix-ins, mix until just combined
Scoop 1 tbsp of mixture and roll between hands into ball
Place on cookie sheet and repeat until all mixture is used (makes about 20-28)
Place cookie sheet in refrigerator for about 30 minutes to set
Store in airtight container in refrigerator for up to 1 week

Add red pepper flakes, Chinese five-spice, and stir for another 30 seconds, until spices are bright. Immediately add the tamari, stir and bring to an aggressive simmer for 3-5 minutes. As the sauce simmers, mix cornstarch and water in a small bowl to make a slurry. Add slurry into the sauce and simmer...

Mix often. The goal with this step is to sear and develop a golden brown color for the seitan. Once the seitan has caramelized, add the sauce to the pan and stir. Add the Trifecta bell peppers. Coat seitan and veggies evenly and cook for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and keep warm until needed.
Warm up the rice in the microwave for about 2-2:30 minutes, along with the Trifecta broccoli.
Portioning in 4 bowls, plate in each ½ cup of Trifecta rice, ¼ cup of broccoli, and 4.5 ounces of tempeh mix. Garnish with ¼ avocado per bowl, sesame seeds and fresh cilantro.

ingredients: 1/2 cup roasted red pepper, drained from jar
1 can kidney beans, drained and rinsed
4 tablespoons orange juice or apple cider vinegar
alternatives.recipe:
In a saucepot on medium-high heat, add avocado oil or olive oil, minced garlic, diced onion, celery, and carrot. Stirring occasionally cook for 3 to 5 minutes or until the onions begin to brown.
Once the meat and vegetables have browned, add in the spices and roasted red peppers and cook for 1 minute until aromatic and stir to combine. Stir in the crumbled Trifecta meat patties and cook for 3 minutes or add 16 ounces of beyond meat and cook for 5 minutes.
Continue to cook on medium-high heat and add vegetable stock to release any of the brown bits stuck to the pan, then stir in the diced tomatoes and beans; bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer cook for 10 minutes. Season with orange juice or apple cider vinegar, a pinch of salt or pepper is desired then dig in!

Put berries in a cast iron skillet or oven safe dish
Bake for 15 minutes
While berries are baking, mix together almond flour, protein powder and 1 tbsp melted coconut oil until crumbly
After 15 minutes, remove berries from oven and top with crumble mixture
Bake 3-5 minutes or until golden
Serve topped with whipped cream or greek yogurt

alternatives.name: Simple Quinoa Bowl Recipe
alternatives.recipe:
Warm quinoa in a microwave for about 1 minute, until lightly warmed (close to room temperature). Add to a large mixing bowl, stirring to break it up.
Drain broccoli and add to the bowl. Chop any large pieces into smaller, bite-sized pieces.
Coarsely chop almonds and add to the bowl.
Chop scallions and parsley and add to quinoa.
Chop figs into small pieces and add to the mixture.
Season with olive oil and lemon juice.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Finish with nutritional yeast and enjoy!

alternatives.name: Ethiopian Injera Bread
alternatives.recipe:
In a large bowl, mix both flours, salt, and baking soda together. Whisk in the club soda until smooth. Then add the vinegar and whisk.
Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Pour oil on a paper towel and wipe the skillet with the oiled paper towel. This creates a thin fatty layer to make it easier to flip the crepes.
Using a scoop, pour batter into the skillet creating a 6-inch circle. Quickly and carefully swirl the pan around to thin out the batter until it measures 8- to 9-inches across.
Cook for 1 minute, then using a large spatula, flip the Injera over and cook another minute. Remove from the skillet and stack on a plate. Repeat with remaining batter. The Injera will seem slightly crisp in the pan, but will soften immediately when placed on the plate. The stacking also helps steam and soften the Injera sourdough crepes.
Once finished cooking the Injera. Cut the circles in half with a pizza cutter, roll into tubes and stack. Keep warm until ready to serve. Serve the Injera with Doro Wat or Mesir Wat, tearing pieces of Injera and using it to pick up the Doro Wat.

Add garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add smoked paprika, cardamom seeds, cumin, coriander and Scotch Bonnet pepper. Cook for a few minutes to bloom the spices.
Add collard greens and give it a stir.
Cover pot and cook on medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add salt and stir to combine.

alternatives.name: Ethiopian Spris
alternatives.recipe:
Cut the papaya in half and then into 1/4. Chop the 1/4 section and set it aside.
Cut and chop the mangoes and avocadoes and set them aside.
Place each of the fruits into three large different cups to blend with an immersion blender.
Add 1/3 cup of sugar to each cup, 1/3 cup of cold water, 1/3 cup of ice, and 1 tablespoon of lime juice. Blend it until smooth. All three juices should be smooth and creamy.
To layer, start by spooning the papaya mixture into a glass. Next, spoon the avocado mixture, and finally, spoon the mango mixture on top. You do not want to simply pour the mixtures on top on top of each other as they can sometimes sink into each other.
Garnish with lime zest and mint if desired. Enjoy!

Grease an 8×8 inch baking dish with olive oil. Give teff a good stir and pour into the baking dish. Using an oiled spatula, smooth the top. Let this cool and place into the fridge until it sets up and is firm, about 4 hours (or overnight). If making ahead see notes.
Once teff is cooled and firm, cut into four squares. Remove cakes, then cut each into two triangles. ( See notes, for appetizer version.)
Pan fry the teff cakes in a skillet, with a little oil, over medium heat, until crispy and heated through, about 5-6 minutes on each side. Place in a warm oven if you like. (Alternately, you can bake these on a greased, parchment-lined sheet pan- 20 mins @400F . Brush the tops with olive oil. )
Wipe out the skillet, heat 1 tablespoon oil over medium heat and add garlic, stirring until golden, about 2-3 mintues. Add Kale and gently wilt. Add a splash of white wine and season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
Place tomatoes, parsley, olive oil, and balsamic in a small bowl and mix. Season with salt and pepper.
Place the warm, crispy teff cakes ( 2 triangles per person) on a plate, layering with the chard. Divide the tomato relish over the top, spooning any remaining dressing on or around the cakes. Serve immediately!

alternatives.name: Teff Porridge
ingredients: generous 3-finger pinch of and ground warming spice- like ground clove, cardamom, nutmeg, or cinnamon, allspice or even 5 spice.
generous 3 finger pinch of kosher salt
fresh fruit- sliced figs or berries or pears
2 tablespoons crushed, toasted walnuts- or any other nut.
alternatives.recipe:
Over medium-high heat, In a small pot, toast teff until it begins to pop, and smell nutty- stirring often, about 3 minutes.
Once you hear its adorable popping, add water and spices and salt.
Bring to a boil, cover and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes. Give a quick stir and at this point,
If it seems too thick, add a bit more water, to get it to your desired consistency. Cook a couple more minutes.
Divide among 2 bowls and top with sliced figs, walnuts and a generous drizzle of honey.

alternatives.name: Ethiopian Cabbage
ingredients: ½ cup (120ml) olive oil (or canola oil)
½ teaspoon (1g) cayenne pepper ((more or less to taste))
8 cups (600g) chopped cabbage ((about ½ cabbage head))
alternatives.recipe:
Heat oil over medium heat in a large saucepan or Dutch oven.
Add the onions and let them saute for a minute or two, then add the carrots, potatoes, and tomatoes.
Stir in the spices: garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, curry, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Simmer for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
Pour about 1 cup of water into the saucepan (add more as needed). Simmer for 10-15 minutes until the potatoes are almost tender.
Finally, add cabbage and green pepper, stir, and continue cooking for about 5 minutes.
Adjust for seasonings. Serve hot.

alternatives.name: Vegan Tofu Tabbouleh Bowl Recipe
ingredients: 3-4 small beets, roasted at home, or store-bought sliced
or 1 package of firm tofu cut into cubes
alternatives.recipe:
Prepare your fried tofu, tabbouleh, and quinoa according to recipe directions. Combine the quinoa and 2 cups tabbouleh together to marinade. You should have extra tabbouleh for finishing your bowl.
Wash your vegetables and greens. Remove the stems from the kale and finely chop. Using a vegetable peeler, peel the carrot into fine strips. Toss the kale, carrots, and chickpeas together and set aside.
Remove the peel from your orange and slice into slices. Do this over a bowl to save any orange juice and mix it into the tabbouleh.
To build your bowl: measure 1/3 cup tabbouleh and quinoa mixture, 4 ounces tofu, 1/2 cup roasted beets, 3-4 sliced orange, 1 cup kale, carrot, and chickpea salad. Top with extra tabbouleh if desired.

alternatives.name: Ethiopian Yataklete kilkil
ingredients: ¼ cup or more canola oil
½ teaspoon curry powder or turmeric spice
½ teaspoon white or black pepper
1 teaspoon salt, (plus more adjust to suit taste buds)
alternatives.recipe:
Add potatoes in a large saucepan, salt, and add water just enough to cover potatoes. Boil until tender - about 10 minutes or more. Remove and drain water.
In a large saucepan or Dutch oven, heat oil over medium heat. Add onions, give it a minute or two then stir in all the spices; garlic, Jalapenos peppers, ginger, coriander, smoked paprika, curry, white pepper, and chili pepper. Simmer for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent burns. Then add tomatoes and bring to a boil and simmer for another 5 minutes.
Then add potatoes, green beans, and carrots. Season with salt.
Continue to simmer for about 10 minutes; stirring frequently to allow the flavors to blend. If needed, add water to prevent any burns.
Adjust for seasonings. Serve warm.

1 cup Trifecta mixed veg or broccoli
1 avocado, sliced lengthwise, divided into quarters

For sauce: In a small saucepan, heat 2 teaspoons of avocado oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and ginger, and stir often for about 1 minute or until a dark golden color begins to form. Add red pepper flakes, Chinese five-spice, and stir for another 30 seconds, until spices are bright. Immediately add the tamari, stir and bring to an aggressive simmer for 3-5 minutes. As the sauce simmers, mix cornstarch and water in a small bowl to make a slurry. Add slurry into the sauce and simmer...
Heat remaining 1 teaspoon of avocado oil in a large non-stick saute pan over medium-high heat. Add 1 teaspoon of garlic, stir to avoid garlic from burning, and immediately add diced seitan. Mix often. The goal with this step is to sear and develop a golden brown color for the seitan. Once the seitan has caramelized, add the sauce to the pan and stir. Add the Trifecta bell peppers. Coat seitan and veggies evenly and cook for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and keep warm until needed.
Warm up the rice in the microwave for about 2-2:30 minutes, along with the Trifecta broccoli.
Portioning in 4 bowls, plate in each ½ cup of Trifecta rice, ¼ cup of broccoli, and 4.5 ounces of tempeh mix. Garnish with ¼ avocado per bowl, sesame seeds and fresh cilantro.

alternatives.name: Vegan BBQ Tofu Stir Fry Recipe
ingredients: 1 tablespoon Tamari (gluten-free soy sauce), low sodium preferred
3 each Garlic, fresh, grated or 2 teaspoon garlic powder
1 teaspoon Ginger, fresh, grated, or 3 teaspoons ginger powder
alternatives.recipe:
Prepare the BBQ sauce by combining all ingredients in a bowl. Whisk to incorporate. Keep on the side.
Drain tofu and press by placing paper towels in the top and bottom of the tofu and placing a hefty book carefully on top of the tofu. Use a small sheet tray if needed to capture the water drainage. Press for at least 5 minutes, discard paper towels, and then dice block evenly. Transfer to a bowl. Season tofu with cornstarch, 2 tablespoons of the BBQ sauce, and a pinch of salt. Mix with hands to coat tofu evenly. Set aside.
Heat up avocado oil on a non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Once the oil is slightly smoking (about 30-45 seconds of constant heat), add the tofu. You want to hear a sear go on as we are looking at caramelizing the tofu and browning it on most sides. Do this for about 2-3 minutes, stirring as necessary. Add the vegetables, along with the quinoa and brown rice. Saute for another 2-3 minutes until heated through. Lastly, add the sauce into the pan and mix to coat all ingredients evenly. The s...
Conversely, if you are tracking your macros closely, heat up the quinoa separately in a bowl in the microwave, covered with a damp paper towel for 2 minutes. Do the same with the Trifecta vegetables. Still reheat the tofu in the pan by searing it over medium-high heat with avocado oil and the sauce.
Portion ⅔ cup of quinoa, ½ cup of Trifecta veggies, and 4 ounce of the saucy tofu on a bowl. Garnish with fresh herbs like cilantro, and some sesame seeds.
Enjoy!

alternatives.name: Easy Beefy Flatbread Recipe
ingredients: ½ cup store-bought slaw mix or thinly sliced cabbage (use a mandolin)
alternatives.recipe:
Preheat toaster oven or larger oven to 350F. Slightly toast the flatbreads to help them keep their bodies and avoid becoming soggy while constructing them. Spray a sheet tray with oil and spread all four flatbreads on the tray, making sure they are not overlapping each other. Toast for 3 minutes, keeping an eye on the flatbreads. Remove from the oven carefully. Keep oven on.
Microwave the Trifecta beef patties covered completely by a damp paper cloth towel, for a minute and a half. Remove from microwave and after the patties are slightly cooled down, crumble the patties to its original form - ground beef - with your hands into a separate bowl. Keep warm.
Spread 1 tablespoon of pesto on top of each flatbread.
Divide ¼ cup of arugula and ¼ cup of Trifecta mushrooms on top of each flatbread, sprinkling around.
Similarly, sprinkle 4 ounces of the crumbled beef on each flatbread. Finish by dividing the 1 ounce of crumbled gorgonzola cheese evenly into all 4 flatbreads (¼ ounce on each).
Return built flatbreads to the sheet tray used to toast the flatbreads, and bake in the oven for 3-5 minutes, or until cheese is melted. Cut into quarters and enjoy!

alternatives.name: Homemade Vegetarian Chili Recipe
ingredients: 1/2 cup roasted red pepper, drained from jar
1 can kidney beans, drained and rinsed
4 tablespoons orange juice or apple cider vinegar
alternatives.recipe:
In a saucepot on medium-high heat, add avocado oil or olive oil, minced garlic, diced onion, celery, and carrot. Stirring occasionally cook for 3 to 5 minutes or until the onions begin to brown.
Once the meat and vegetables have browned, add in the spices and roasted red peppers and cook for 1 minute until aromatic and stir to combine. Stir in the crumbled Trifecta meat patties and cook for 3 minutes or add 16 ounces of beyond meat and cook for 5 minutes.
Continue to cook on medium-high heat and add vegetable stock to release any of the brown bits stuck to the pan, then stir in the diced tomatoes and beans; bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer cook for 10 minutes. Season with orange juice or apple cider vinegar, a pinch of salt or pepper is desired then dig in!

alternatives.name: Ethiopian Gomen Wat Recipe
alternatives.recipe:
In a large saucepan or pot, melt the Niter Kibbeh.
Add onion and saute on medium low heat until the onions are very soft, about 5-8 minutes.
Add garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add smoked paprika, cardamom seeds, cumin, coriander and Scotch Bonnet pepper. Cook for a few minutes to bloom the spices.
Add collard greens and give it a stir.
Cover pot and cook on medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add salt and stir to combine.

alternatives.name: Cheesy Vegan Tofu Scramble Recipe
ingredients: 14 ounces firm tofu, drained and pressed
½ cup Trifecta green bell pepper
½ cup Trifecta portobello mushrooms, diced
alternatives.recipe:
Crumble tofu into a large bowl.
Stir in spices and nutritional yeast, 1 to 2 Tbsp of olive oil, and let sit for five minutes.
Add crumbled tofu to the skillet and sauté for 5 minutes, until tofu is lightly golden and firm.
Add in veggies and stir for another 3 to 4 minutes, mixing flavors together until spinach is wilted.
Season with salt and pepper and serve immediately.

alternatives.name: Vegetarian Sweet Potato and Greens Breakfast Hash Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups thinly chopped collard greens, dandelion greens, spinach, or kale
alternatives.recipe:
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add two tablespoons of coconut oil. Add in the small diced sweet potato and cook for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally. The potatoes will start to brown and crisp up. While the potatoes are cooking, move on to step 2.
Remove the tofu from its packaging and crumbIe it into a small bowl. Add the nutritional yeast, onion powder, garlic powder, black pepper, paprika, and pinch of salt. Mix to combine then set aside.
Once the potatoes start to get tender and browned, add in the diced bell pepper and greens if you are using a hearty green like collard greens or kale. Saute the potatoes and peppers for 3 minutes. If you are using any other roasted vegetables, add them right now as well. If you are using leafy greens such spinach, dandelion greens, or arugula wait to add those at the very end, after the tofu, as they will cook extremely fast. If the potatoes and peppers start to stick to the bottom of the sk...
Add the tofu and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally until the tofu is firm and cooked through. Season with salt and pepper, and a squeeze of lemon. Then, dig in!

alternatives.name: Low Carb Berry Cobbler Crumble Recipe
alternatives.recipe:
Preheat oven to 375 degrees F
Toss berries with xanthum gum, lemon zest and any sweetener, if using
Put berries in a cast iron skillet or oven safe dish
Bake for 15 minutes
While berries are baking, mix together almond flour, protein powder and 1 tbsp melted coconut oil until crumbly
After 15 minutes, remove berries from oven and top with crumble mixture
Bake 3-5 minutes or until golden
Serve topped with whipped cream or greek yogurt

alternatives.name: Simple Quinoa Bowl Recipe

Drain broccoli and add to the bowl. Chop any large pieces into smaller, bite-sized pieces.
Coarsely chop almonds and add to the bowl.
Chop scallions and parsley and add to quinoa.
Chop figs into small pieces and add to the mixture.
Season with olive oil and lemon juice.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Finish with nutritional yeast and enjoy!

alternatives.name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Kaged Muscle Kasein Balls
ingredients: 2 cups pitted packed soft Medjool dates
1 scoop Kaged Muscle Kasein chocolate protein powder
Chocolate Coating: 1 cup unsweetened dark chocolate finely chopped, 1.5 tbsp coconut oil
Peanut Butter Drizzle: 3 tbsp natural peanut butter, 1 tbsp coconut oil, 0.5 tbsp maple syrup
alternatives.recipe:
Process dates in food processor until smooth. Add peanut butter, vanilla, protein powder and salt, process 30 seconds
Transfer mixture to freezer for 30 minutes to set
Remove from freezer, scoop tablespoons of mixture, roll into 1-inch balls, place on lined baking sheet
Freeze balls 15 minutes to set
Melt chocolate and coconut oil in a double boiler until smooth
Spoon tablespoon of chocolate mixture over each ball, freeze 15 minutes
Whisk peanut butter, coconut oil and maple syrup in double boiler until smooth
Drizzle balls with peanut butter mixture and serve

alternatives.name: Vegan Pesto Recipe
alternatives.recipe:
Lightly roast the pistachios at 425 for 3-5 minutes, checking oven constantly. Remove from the oven and let cool slightly.
In a food processor, combine garlic, toasted pistachios, and 2 tablespoons olive oil. Process scraping sides as needed, until a paste is formed.
Add basil and mint to the food processor and pulse to incorporate basil into the paste.
Add lemon juice and process one last time, scraping the sides again as needed.
Remove basil paste into a bowl and fold remaining olive oil into the pistachio paste using a spatula.
Sprinkle a pinch of salt and mix to incorporate. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Enjoy!

alternatives.name: Romesco Sauce Recipe
alternatives.recipe:
Roast the tomato (unpeeled) and pepper at 425°F for 20 minutes.
While your tomato and pepper roast, roast almonds and garlic on a separate sheet pan for 3 to 5 minutes.
Let cool and then remove the skin from tomato and peel the garlic.
In a food processor add your almonds, peeled tomato, roasted red pepper, olive oil, paprika, cayenne pepper, and vinegar.
Season with salt and cayenne pepper to taste.
Enjoy!

alternatives.name: Curried Cauliflower Quinoa Recipe
alternatives.recipe:
Dice the scallion and bell pepper into small bite sized pieces. Then wash, de-stem and mince the parsley.
In a large mixing bowl, combine quinoa, cauliflower and chopped veggies. Add in raisins and parsley.
Stir in curry powder to evenly coat everything. Add some chili flakes to increase spiciness.
Add olive oil and lemon juice, and season with salt to taste.
Enjoy!

alternatives.name: Ethiopian Gomen Wat Recipe
alternatives.recipe:
In a large saucepan or pot, melt the Niter Kibbeh.
Add onion and saute on medium low heat until the onions are very soft, about 5-8 minutes.
Add garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add smoked paprika, cardamom seeds, cumin, coriander and Scotch Bonnet pepper. Cook for a few minutes to bloom the spices.
Add collard greens and give it a stir.
Cover pot and cook on medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add salt and stir to combine.

alternatives.name: High Protein Pesto Pasta Recipe
ingredients: High Protein pasta made from chickpea, peas, or lentils
½ Cup Sun-dried tomatoes, packed in oil, drained
For the Pesto: (makes about 1 ½ cups)
1 Cup Packed parsley leaves, stems on!
⅓ Cup Olive oil or avocado oil (less bitter)

Preheat the empty pasta pot over medium heat. Add the artichokes and Sundried tomatoes. Cook for 2 minutes. Add the Spinach and cook slightly, about 1-2 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the cooked pasta, pesto, and pasta water, stir, and plate.
Garnish with vegan parmesan or toasted sunflower seeds if desired.

alternatives.recipe:
Place all ingredients into a blender
Blend until smooth
Serve immediately

alternatives.name: Nichole Ohrablo's Super Shake Recipe
alternatives.recipe:
Add all ingredients to blender
Blend until smooth
Serve and enjoy

alternatives.name: Teff Porridge
ingredients: generous 3-finger pinch of and ground warming spice- like ground clove, cardamom, nutmeg, or cinnamon, allspice or even 5 spice.
generous 3 finger pinch of kosher salt
fresh fruit- sliced figs or berries or pears
2 tablespoons crushed, toasted walnuts- or any other nut.
alternatives.recipe:
Over medium-high heat, In a small pot, toast teff until it begins to pop, and smell nutty- stirring often, about 3 minutes.
Once you hear its adorable popping, add water and spices and salt.
Bring to a boil, cover and simmer on low heat for 10 minutes. Give a quick stir and at this point,
If it seems too thick, add a bit more water, to get it to your desired consistency. Cook a couple more minutes.
Divide among 2 bowls and top with sliced figs, walnuts and a generous drizzle of honey.

alternatives.name: Vegetarian Sweet Potato and Greens Breakfast Hash Recipe
ingredients: 1.5 cups thinly chopped collard greens, dandelion greens, spinach, or kale
alternatives.recipe:
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and add two tablespoons of coconut oil. Add in the small diced sweet potato and cook for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally. The potatoes will start to brown and crisp up. While the potatoes are cooking, move on to step 2.
Remove the tofu from its packaging and crumbIe it into a small bowl. Add the nutritional yeast, onion powder, garlic powder, black pepper, paprika, and pinch of salt. Mix to combine then set aside.
Once the potatoes start to get tender and browned, add in the diced bell pepper and greens if you are using a hearty green like collard greens or kale. Saute the potatoes and peppers for 3 minutes. If you are using any other roasted vegetables, add them right now as well. If you are using leafy greens such spinach, dandelion greens, or arugula wait to add those at the very end, after the tofu, as they will cook extremely fast. If the potatoes and peppers start to stick to the bottom of the sk...
Add the tofu and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally until the tofu is firm and cooked through. Season with salt and pepper, and a squeeze of lemon. Then, dig in!

alternatives.name: Low Carb Berry Cobbler Crumble Recipe
alternatives.recipe:
Preheat oven to 375 degrees F
Toss berries with xanthum gum, lemon zest and any sweetener, if using
Put berries in a cast iron skillet or oven safe dish
Bake for 15 minutes
While berries are baking, mix together almond flour, protein powder and 1 tbsp melted coconut oil until crumbly
After 15 minutes, remove berries from oven and top with crumble mixture
Bake 3-5 minutes or until golden
Serve topped with whipped cream or greek yogurt

alternatives.name: Berbere Potatoes
ingredients: 1.5 lbs thin-skinned potatoes, thinly sliced (yukons, red, etc)
alternatives.recipe:
Preheat oven to 400F
In a cast iron or oven-proof skillet, heat the oil over medium heat, add the shallots and saute, stirring until tender, 4-5 minutes. Add the garlic and cook 2 minutes, until fragrant. Add the tomatoes and their juices, and continue cooking until they begin to break down and soften, 3-4 minutes. Stir in the Berbere spice and the water.
Scoop out ¾ cup of the mixture and set aside.
Add the potatoes tossing to coat each side and layer them. Pour the remaining tomato mixture over top.
Cover tightly with a lid or with foil. Bring to a hard simmer on the stovetop, then place in the oven for 30 minutes covered.
Remove lid, checking that potatoes are fork-tender, drizzle with olive oil, bake an additional 10 minutes uncovered.
Scatter with fresh parsley. Enjoy!

alternatives.name: Ethiopian Injera Bread
alternatives.recipe:
In a large bowl, mix both flours, salt, and baking soda together. Whisk in the club soda until smooth. Then add the vinegar and whisk.
Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Pour oil on a paper towel and wipe the skillet with the oiled paper towel. This creates a thin fatty layer to make it easier to flip the crepes.
Using a scoop, pour batter into the skillet creating a 6-inch circle. Quickly and carefully swirl the pan around to thin out the batter until it measures 8- to 9-inches across.
Cook for 1 minute, then using a large spatula, flip the Injera over and cook another minute. Remove from the skillet and stack on a plate. Repeat with remaining batter. The Injera will seem slightly crisp in the pan, but will soften immediately when placed on the plate. The stacking also helps steam and soften the Injera sourdough crepes.
Once finished cooking the Injera. Cut the circles in half with a pizza cutter, roll into tubes and stack. Keep warm until ready to serve. Serve the Injera with Doro Wat or Mesir Wat, tearing pieces of Injera and using it to pick up the Doro Wat.

alternatives.name: Authentic Injera (Ethiopian Flatbread)
ingredients: 2 cups teff flour, brown or ivory (, or substitute a portion of it with some barley or wheat flour (note: teff is gluten free))
Note: If you're new to making injera I recommend using a combination of teff and barley or wheat as 100% teff is more challenging to work with.
3 cups distilled water ((fluoride and chlorine will both interfere with the fermentation process))
Note: This method involves wild yeast fermentation. See blog post for details about using commercial yeast as a starter
alternatives.recipe:
See blog post for detailed instructions*NOTE: Using mostly or all teff (which is the traditional Ethiopian way) will NOT produce the spongy, fluffy injera served in most restaurants which are adapted to the western palate and use mostly wheat, sometimes a little barley, and occasionally a little teff added in.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and water (and yeast if you're using it). Loosely place some plastic wrap on the bowl (it needs some air circulation, you just want to keep any critters out) and let the mixture sit undisturbed at room temperature for 4-5 days (the longer it ferments, the deeper the flavor). (Depending on what kind of flour you're using, you may need to add a little more water if the mixture is becoming dry.) The mixture will be fizzy, the color will be very...
In a small saucepan, bring 1 cup of water to a boil. Stir in 1/2 cup of the injera batter, whisking constantly until it is thickened. This will happen pretty quickly. Then stir the cooked/thickened batter back into the original fermented batter. Add some water to the batter to thin it out to the consistency of crepe batter. I added about 2/3 cup water but this will vary from batch to batch. The batter will have a sweet-soured nutty smell.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Depending on how good your non-stick pan is, you may need to very lightly spray it with some oil. Spread the bottom of the skillet with the injera batter - not as thin as crepes but not as thick as traditional pancakes. Allow the injera to bubble and let the bubbles pop. Once the bubbles have popped, place a lid on top of the pan and turn off the heat. Let the injera steam cook for a couple or so more minutes until cooked through. Be careful not to o...
IMPORTANT NOTE:  Both the texture and color of the injera will vary greatly depending on what kind of teff you use (dark or ivory) and whether or not you're combining it with other flours.  Gluten-based flours (e.g. wheat and barley) will yield a much different texture than 100% teff.  In the pictures and recipe below I'm using 100% dark teff, something you will not find in restaurants and will look different than what most are accustomed to, but is traditional to Ethiopian hom...

alternatives.name: Vegan High Protein Veggie Pasta Recipe
alternatives.recipe:
Cook pasta al dente. Let cool and set aside.
Dice scallions and coarsely chop parsley for garnish. Set aside.
Combine pasta and veggies in a large bowl.
Add oil, lemon zest, and seasoning.
Finish with parsley and scallion.
Enjoy!

alternatives.name: Azefa - Ethiopian Lentil Salad
alternatives.recipe:
Bring lentils and 4 cups of water to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer and cook for 30 minutes, until lentils are just tender.
Drain immediately and run with cold water.
Using a coffee/spice grinder, grind mustard seed, black pepper and cayenne pepper to a fine powder.
Transfer the spice powder to a bowl and add boiling water. Stir to combine.
Add lemon juice and slowly drizzle in olive oil, while whisking constantly.
In a large bowl, combine lentils, bell pepper, cilantro and salt.
Add dressing and stir to combine.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.

alternatives.name: Tempeh Garlic Ginger Soy Bowl Recipe
ingredients: 16 ounces Tempeh, diced into bite sizes
1 cup Trifecta mixed veg or broccoli
1 avocado, sliced lengthwise, divided into quarters
alternatives.recipe:
For sauce: In a small saucepan, heat 2 teaspoons of avocado oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and ginger, and stir often for about 1 minute or until a dark golden color begins to form. Add red pepper flakes, Chinese five-spice, and stir for another 30 seconds, until spices are bright. Immediately add the tamari, stir and bring to an aggressive simmer for 3-5 minutes. As the sauce simmers, mix cornstarch and water in a small bowl to make a slurry. Add slurry into the sauce and simmer...
Heat remaining 1 teaspoon of avocado oil in a large non-stick saute pan over medium-high heat. Add 1 teaspoon of garlic, stir to avoid garlic from burning, and immediately add diced seitan. Mix often. The goal with this step is to sear and develop a golden brown color for the seitan. Once the seitan has caramelized, add the sauce to the pan and stir. Add the Trifecta bell peppers. Coat seitan and veggies evenly and cook for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and keep warm until needed.
Warm up the rice in the microwave for about 2-2:30 minutes, along with the Trifecta broccoli.
Portioning in 4 bowls, plate in each ½ cup of Trifecta rice, ¼ cup of broccoli, and 4.5 ounces of tempeh mix. Garnish with ¼ avocado per bowl, sesame seeds and fresh cilantro.

alternatives.name: Low-Carb Portobello Mushroom Bruschetta
alternatives.recipe:
First, heat up a skillet or a grill. Then spray the Mushrooms with non stick spray. Grill for 2-3 minutes on each side. Remove from heat but keep the stove/ grill on.
In a bowl, mix the tomatoes, garlic, balsamic vinegar, and basil together. Add the mix to the inside of the mushrooms.
Top the mushrooms with cheese and place back on the skillet/grill.
Cover and heat for 2-3 minutes or until the cheese has melted. Enjoy!
